claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions. Remember, Child Benefit is assessed for each child individually.

First, let me ask: **Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, how old are they?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child. My son Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. So you have an 8-year-old son named Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), he would automatically meet the age requirement. Now I need to ask a few more questions about your circumstances.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all. He's only 8, so he's just in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes sense - thank you for confirming.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Next question: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Good. Now, **is Alex in any type of apprenticeship program?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's only 8, so he's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask about your circumstances as the claimant.

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now, regarding Alex:

**Do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask:

**Do you contribute to Alex's upkeep?** This could include paying for things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or making maintenance payments under a court order or agreement.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out a bit - I contribute towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess if you're responsible for Alex, I need to understand the level of your contributions. 

**Do you pay at least the equivalent of what Child Benefit would be** (or provide equivalent support in kind) **towards Alex's care?** This could include regular payments for food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, or maintenance payments.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, I help out when I can, but I'm not sure if it's quite that much. What's the Child Benefit amount?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
The current Child Benefit rate for an eldest or only child is approximately £25.60 per week (though rates can change, so it's worth checking the latest on gov.uk). For additional children, it's approximately £16.95 per week each.

So for Alex, to be considered responsible for him through financial contributions, you would need to be contributing at least around £25.60 per week (or about £110 per month) towards his upkeep. This can include things like maintenance payments, money for food, clothes, pocket money, housing costs, or other support.

**Are you contributing at least this amount regularly towards Alex's care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not contributing that much. I give about £10 a week to help with things like clothes and bits and pieces, but it's not as much as £25.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being honest about that. I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I need to explain the situation regarding Child Benefit eligibility.

To be eligible to claim Child Benefit, you must be **responsible for the child**. According to the official rules, this means either:
- **Living with the child**, OR
- **Contributing at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount** towards their upkeep (approximately £25.60 per week)

Since Alex doesn't live with you and your contributions (£10 per week) are below the Child Benefit threshold, you would **not meet the responsibility requirement** for Child Benefit.

**Important to note:** If Alex lives with someone else who is responsible for him (such as his other parent or a guardian who lives with him), **that person may be eligible to claim Child Benefit** for Alex instead.

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any time, and it's usually the person who has the main day-to-day responsibility for the child - typically the person they live with.

**Would you like me to provide information about who might be eligible to claim for Alex, or do you have any other questions?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Oh, I see. Yes, he lives with his mum, so I suppose she would be the one who could claim it then. That makes sense.

Thank you for explaining that to me.
